questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In Young's double slit experiment, an interference pattern is obtained on a screen by a light of wavelength $6000 Å$ coming from the coherent sources $S_1$ and $S_2$. At certain point $P$ on the screen third dark fringe is formed. Then, the path difference $S_1 P-S_2 P$ in microns is:
questions.solution:
$\lambda=6000 Å=6 \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{~m}$
Path difference for dark fringe;
$\Delta x=(2 n+1) \lambda / 2$
For third dark fringe, $n=2$
$\therefore \quad \Delta x=(2 \times 2+1) \times \frac{6 \times 10^{-7}}{2}$
$=\frac{5 \times 6 \times 10^{-7}}{2}$
$=15 \times 10^{-7}$
$=1.5 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~m}$
$=1.5 \mu \mathrm{m}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Light waves producing interference have their amplitudes in the ratio $3: 2$. The intensity ratio of maximum and minimum of interference fringes is
questions.solution:
Ratio of amplitudes, $\frac{a_1}{a_2}=\frac{3}{2}$
$\therefore \quad \begin{aligned} a_1 & =3 k, a_2=2 k \\ \frac{I_{\max }}{I_{\operatorname{mix}}} & =\frac{\left(a_1+a_2\right)^2}{\left(a_1-a_2\right)^2} \\ & =\frac{(3 k+2 k)^2}{(3 k-2 k)^2}=\frac{25}{1} \\ I_{\operatorname{man}}: I_{\min } & =25: 1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics

questions.q: In Young's double-slit experiment, in an interference pattern, the second minimum is observed exactly in front of one slit. The distance between the slits is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>d</mi></math> and the distance between source and screen is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>D</mi></math>. The wavelength of the light source used is
questions.solution:
Distance of second minima from central point  is y=d2.

We know that minima is given by,

dsinθ= n-12λdyD= 2-12λ

d 2=Dd2×2-12λ
∴d2=Dd×32λ
∴λ=d23D

questions.q: In the adjacent diagram, CP represents a wavefront and AO & BP, the corresponding two rays. Find the condition of θ for constructive interference at P between the ray BP and reflected ray OP.
questions.solution:
PR=d
 ∴ PO=dsecθ
and CO=POcos2θ=dsecθcos2θ 
Path difference between the two rays is,
 Δx=PO+OC =dsecθ+dsecθcos2θ
phase difference between the two rays is
Δϕ=π oneisreflected,whileanotherisdirect
Therefore, condition for constructive interference should be

   Δ x = λ 2 , 3 λ 2 ......
or,dsecθ1+cos2θ=λ2
or,   dcosθ2cos2θ=λ2
or,    cosθ=λ4d

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Assertion: A spherical wavefront is produced by a point source of light.

Reason: It is because, the locus of all points, which are equidistant from the point source, is a sphere.
options: Assertion is true, reason is true; reason is a correct explanation for assertion.
Assertion is true, reason is true; reason is not a correct explanation for assertion.
Assertion is true, reason is false.
Assertion is false, reason is true.
questions.solution:
Huygens proposed that light is a longitudinal wave in the aether medium (-medium pervading the entire universe). The set of light particles (- “corpuscles” as called by Newton) oscillating in phase with each other is called a wavefront. Point sources give out spherical wavefronts, while sources at infinity give plane wavefronts.

Hence, the assertion is true.

The reason of these wavefronts to be sphere is because of the locus of points on wavefront which are equidistant from the point source.

Thus, given reason is the correct explanation of the assertion.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In a Young's double slit experiment, a laser light of wave length $560 \mathrm{~nm}$ produces an interference pattern with consecutive bright fringe's separation of $7.2 \mathrm{~mm}$. Now another light is used to produce an interference pattern with consecutive bright fringes' separation of $8.1 \mathrm{~mm}$. The wavelength of second light is
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Select the WRONG statement.
options: According to Huygens" principle, every point on a given wavefront is regarded as a secondary source.
The new disturbance from secondary source travels in all directions with the velocity of light and is called secondary wavelets.
The surface of tangency to secondary wavelets in forward direction only gives new wavefront.
Huygens could explain rectilinear propagation of light.
questions.solution:
Checking the given options:

  1. According to Huygens' principle, every point on a given wavefront is regarded as a secondary source. This statement is correct.
  2. The new disturbance from secondary source travels in all directions with the velocity of light and is called secondary wavelets. This statement is correct.
  3. The surface of tangency to secondary wavelets in forward direction only gives new wavefront. This statement is correct.
  4. Huygens could explain rectilinear propagation of light. This is the wrong statement. 

Newton proposed that light is made up of particles (he called them corpuscles) which comes out of the source in a straight line, explaining the rectilinear propagation of light.

Huygens, on the other hand, proposed that light is a longitudinal wave. Thus, he supported the wave nature of light.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In Young’s double slit experiment fifth dark fringe is formed opposite to one of the slit. If D is the distance between the slits and the screen and d is the separation between the slits, then the wavelength of light used is
questions.solution:
Position of nth dark fringes in Young’s double slit experiment is given,
xn=Dd2n-1λ2
⇒λ=2xndD2n-1 … (i)
Since, fifth dark fringe is formed opposite to one of the slit,
∴2x5=d (distance between slits)
Put n=5 in Eq. (i), we get
λ=2x5dD2×5-1
=d.dD10-1since,2x5=d
d29D
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: On placing a thin film of mica of thickness $12 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~cm}$ in the path of one of the interfering waves in Young's double slit experiment using monochromatic light, the fringe pattern shifts through a distance equal to width of a bright fringe. If wavelength used is $\lambda=6 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~cm}$, the refractive index of mica is
questions.solution:
Path difference due to the mica film: $(\mu-1) \mathrm{t}$
The shift in the pattern be $\Delta$ yand the corresponding path difference can be written as: $\Delta x=\frac{\Delta y D}{d}=(\mu-1) t$
$\therefore \Delta y=\frac{d}{D}(\mu-1) t$
Fringe width is $\beta=\frac{\lambda D}{d}$, so,
$\Delta y=\frac{\beta}{\lambda}(\mu-1) t$
If the shift is equal to the fringe width $\beta$
$\begin{aligned} & \beta=\frac{\beta}{\lambda}(\mu-1) t \\ & \Rightarrow(\mu-1) t=\lambda \\ & \therefore \mu=\frac{\lambda}{t}+1=\left(\frac{6 \times 10^{-5}}{12 \times 10^{-5}}\right)+1=1.5\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A clear sheet of Polaroid is placed on top of a similar sheet so that their axes make an angle of sin-145 with each other. The ratio of the intensity of the emergent light to that of polarised light is
questions.solution:
Given,

angle between polariser and analyser, θ=sin-145

⇒sinθ=45

∴cosθ=1-sin2θ=35

Intensity of transmitted light is given by law of Malus,

I=I0cos2θ

where I and I0 are the intensities of the polarised and emergent light respectively.

∴II0=cos2θ

⇒II0=352

⇒II0=925

Hence, the required ratio is 9:25.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Interference fringes are obtained in a Young double slit experiment using beam of light consisting two wavelengths <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>500</mn><mo> </mo><mi>nm</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>600</mn><mo> </mo><mi>nm</mi></math>. Bright fringes due to both wavelengths coincide at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math> from the central maximum. If the separation between the slits is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>, then the distance between the screen and plane of the slits is
questions.solution:
We know, y=n1λ1Dd=n2λ2Dd

⇒ n1λ1=n2λ2

⇒ n1500nm=n2600nm

⇒ 5n1=6n2

∴ n1=6, n2=5     ∴  n1≠0, n2≠0

Consider;

y=n1λ1Dd

⇒ 2.5 mm=6×500nm3mm×D

⇒ D=2.5×3×10-66×500×10-9

∴ D=2.5 m
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A polarized light of intensity <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>0</mn></mrow></msub></math> is passed through another polarizer whose pass axis makes an angle of <math><msup><mrow><mn>60</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></mrow></msup></math> with the pass axis of the former. What is the intensity of emerging polarized light from second polarizer?
questions.solution:
By Malus law, I=I0cos2⁡θ

(I = Intensity of emergent polarized light)

Where θ=60o, I= ?

=I0×cos2⁡60o

( I0= Intensity passed through polarizer)

=I0×122=I04
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In Young's double slit experiment, red light of wavelength $6000 Å$ is used and the $n$th bright fringe is obtained at a point $P$ on the screen. Keeping the same setting, the source of light is replaced by green light of wavelength $5000 Å$ and now $(n+1)$ th bright fringe is obtained at the point $P$ on the screen. The value of $n$ is
questions.solution:
Fringe width, $\beta_G=n \frac{\lambda D}{d}$
And for $n$ number of fringes,
$\beta_G=n \frac{\lambda D}{d} \text { or, } \beta_R=n \frac{\lambda D}{d}$
For $(n+1)$ th number of fringes,
$\begin{aligned}
& \beta_G=(n+1) \frac{\lambda D}{d} \\
& \because \beta_{\mathrm{R}}=\beta_{\mathrm{G}} \\
& \text { or, } n \frac{\lambda_R D}{d}=(n+1) \frac{\lambda_R D}{d} \\
& n .6000 \times 10^{-10} \frac{D}{d}=(n+1) 5000 \times 10^{-10} \frac{D}{d} \text { or, } \\
& \Rightarrow 6 n=(n+1) 5 \\
& \Rightarrow 6 n=5 n+5 \text { or, } 6 n-5 n=5 \\
& \therefore \quad n=5
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two coherent point sources $S_1$ and $S_2$ vibrating in phase emit light of wavelength $\lambda$. The separation between them is $2 \lambda$ as shown in figure. The first bright fringe is formed at $P$ due to interference on a screen placed at a distance $D$ from $S_1(D>>\lambda)$, then $O P$ is
questions.solution:
From the figure,
$\begin{aligned} & \left(S_1 P\right)^2=D^2+x^2 \\ & \text { and } \\ & \left(S_2 P\right)^2=(D-2 \lambda)^2+x^2 \\ & \text { So, }\left(S_1 P\right)^2-\left(S_2 P\right)^2=D^2+x^2 \\ & -\left[D^2+4 \lambda^2-4 D \lambda+x^2\right] \\ & =4 D \lambda-4 \lambda^2 \\ & \left(S_1 P+S_2 P\right)\left(S_1 P-S_2 P\right)=4 D \lambda-4 \lambda^2 \\ & {[D>>\lambda]} \\ & \text { Since, }[D>>\lambda] \\ & \therefore \quad S_1 P=S_2 P \\ & \end{aligned}$ and neglecting higher power of $\lambda$ $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad 2 S_1 P\left(S_1 P-S_2 P\right)=4 D \lambda \\ & 2\left(S_1 P\right)(\lambda)=4 D \lambda \\ & \text { or } \quad S_1 P=2 D \\ & \sqrt{D^2+x^2}=2 D \\ & x^2=3 D^2 \\ & x=\sqrt{3} D \\ & \end{aligned}$ Alternative $\begin{gathered}2 \lambda \cos \theta=\lambda \\ \Rightarrow \quad \cos \theta=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow \theta=60^{\circ}, \tan \theta=\frac{x}{D} \\ \sqrt{3}=\frac{x}{D} \Rightarrow x=\sqrt{3} D\end{gathered}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two ideal slits S1 and S2 are at a distance d apart and illuminated by light of wavelength λ, passing through an ideal source slit S, placed on the line through S2, as shown. The distance between the planes of slits and the source slit is D. A screen is held at a distance D from the plane of the slits. The minimum value of d for which there is darkness at O is
questions.solution:
From the above diagram, path difference between the waves reaching at P is, Δ=(SS1-SS2)+S1O-S2O

From the diagram, we can write, 

SS1-SS2=D2+d2-D

and S1O-S2O=D2+d2-D.

So,

Δ=2D2+d2-2D=2D1+d2D212-2D

From binomial expansion, as dD<1, 

∆=2D1+d22D2-2D=d2D 

To form darkness at O, ∆ must be equal to λ2 (minimum value). Then,

d2D=λ2⇒d=λD2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two point sources $S_1$ and $S_2$ separated by a distance $10 \mu \mathrm{m}$ emit light waves of wavelength $4 \mu \mathrm{m}$ in phase. A circular wire of radius $40 \mu \mathrm{m}$ is placed around the sources as shown in figure, then ( $O$ is the centre of the circle and $O S_1=O S_2$ )
options: points $A$ and $B$ are dark and points $C$ and $D$ are bright
points $A$ and $B$ are bright and point $C$ and $D$ are dark
points $A$ and $C$ are dark and points $B$ and $D$ are bright
points $A$ and $C$ are bright and points $B$ and $D$ are dark
questions.solution:
Path difference at point $B$, $$ \Delta x_B=S_1 B-S_2 B=0 $$ and at point $D$, $$ \Delta x_D=S_1 D-S_2 D=0 $$ Phase difference, $$ \phi=\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda} \times(0)=0 $$ So, intensity at points $B$ and $D$ $$ I=I_{\max } \cos ^2(\phi / 2)=I_{\max } $$
So, points $B$ and $D$ are bright. Path difference at point $A$, $$ \Delta x_A=35 \mu \mathrm{m} $$ $$ [40-5] $$ But, figure shows $\Delta x_A=\Delta x_C$ is maximum for given circle. When path difference is maximum, intensity will be minimum. So, points $A$ and $C$ are dark.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
In Young's double slit experiment, the intensity of light at a point on the screen is ${ }^{\prime} \mathrm{K}^{\prime}$ unit for path difference ${ }^{\prime} \lambda^{\prime}$. What would be the intensity at a point if path
difference is $\frac{\lambda^{\prime}}{4}$ ?
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In Young's double slit experiment, the two slits are illuminated by a light beam consisting of wavelengths $4200 Å$ and $5040 Å$. If the distance between the slits is $2.4 \mathrm{~mm}$ and the distance between the slits and the screen is $200 \mathrm{~cm}$, the minimum distance from the central bright fringe to the point where the bright fringes due to both the wavelengths coincide is
questions.solution:
Let $n_1$ fringes of $\lambda_1=4200 Å$ and $n_2$ fringes of $\lambda_2=5040 Å$ wavelength are formed in a fixed distance on screen.

So, $n \lambda=$ constant or $n_1 \lambda_1=n_2 \lambda_2$
For minimum distance to be coincide,
$$
\begin{gathered}
n_1=n+1, n_2=n \\
(n+1) 4200=n(5040) \\
4200 n+4200=5040 n \\
n \\
\text { Required distance, } x=\frac{n_1 \lambda_1 D}{d} \\
\text { Given, } D=200 \mathrm{~cm}=2 \mathrm{~m}, d=2 \cdot 4 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}, \\
n_1=5+1=6, \lambda_1=4200 Å \\
x=\frac{6 \times 4200 \times 10^{-10} \times 2}{24 \times 10^{-3}} \\
x=2.1 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}=2.1 \mathrm{~mm}
\end{gathered}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Choose the incorrect statement.
options: The fringe width in a Young's double slit experiment reduces when the width between the two slits increases.
The central fringe observed for in a single slit diffraction experiment is a bright fringe.
The resolving power of the microscope is reciprocal to the maximum separation of two points seen as distinct.
Polarisation phenomenon is observed only for transverse waves.
questions.solution:
(a) In Young's double slit experiment, the
fringe width is given by
$\beta=\frac{\lambda D}{d} \Rightarrow \beta \propto \frac{1}{d}$
So, if width between two slit $(d)$ increases, the fringe width reduces.
(b) In diffraction due to single slit, the path difference between rays reaching centre of screen is zero, so its intensity at centre is maximum i.e., bright fringe is observed at centre.
(c) The resolving power of microscope is
$=\frac{1}{d_{\min }}=\frac{2 n \sin \beta}{1.22 \lambda}$
So, it is the reciprocal of the minimum separation $\left(d_{\min }\right)$ of two points seen as distinct.
(d) The polarisation is possible only in those waves, that have vibrations in different direction, which is in case of transverse waves. Hence, only statement given in option (c) is incorrect.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A screen is at a distance D=80 cm from a diaphragm having two narrow slits S1 and S2 which are d=2 mm apart. The slit S1 is covered by a transparent sheet of thickness t1=2.5 μm and the slit S2 by another sheet of thickness t2=1.25 μm ​as shown in the figure. Both sheets are made of the same material having a refractive index μm=1.40. Water is filled in space between the diaphragm and the screen. A monochromatic light beam of wavelength λ=5000 Å is incident normally on the diaphragm. Assuming the intensity of the beam to be uniform and slits of equal width, calculate the ratio of intensity at C to the maximum intensity of interference pattern obtained on the screen, where C is the foot of the perpendicular bisector of S1S2. [Refractive index of water, μw=43]
questions.solution:
Optical path = (Refractive Index) × (Geometrical Path Length)

Path Difference at point C on the screen

Δ x = μ m t 1 + μ w S 1 C - t 1 - μ m t 2 + μ w S 2 C - t 2

Δ x = μ m t 1 - t 2 - μ w t 1 + μ w t 2              ( ∵ s 1 c= s 2 c)

Δ x = μ m t 1 - t 2 - μ w t 1 - t 2

Δ x = μ m - μ w t 1 - t 2

Δ x = 1.4 - 4 3 2.5 × 10 - 6 - 1.25 × 10 - 6

Δ x = 0.2 3 × 1.25 × 10 - 6

Δ x = 2.5 3 × 1 0 - 7 m

Δ x = 2500 3 Å= 5000 6 Å

Δ x = λ 6 ,

Phase difference     ϕ = π 3 = 60 ο

at any point on the screen     I net = I 1 + I 2 +2 I 1 I 2  cosϕ

Resultant intensity     I C = I + I + 2 I × I  cos  6 0

          I C = 3I

&          I max =I+I+2I   when   ϕ= 0 o ⇒ cosϕ=+1

          I max = 4I

          I C I max = 3 4
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The angular deviation of 5 th order dark fringe is $12^{\circ}$ in a single slit experiment. If the width of the slit is $9 \mu \mathrm{m}$ then the wavelength of the incident light is
questions.solution:
Given, angular deviation of 5th order dark
fringe, $\theta=12^{\circ}$,
Width of the slit, $d=9 \mu \mathrm{m}$
Now, angular deviation of $n$th fringe $=n \cdot \frac{\lambda}{d}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore 12 \times \frac{\pi}{180} & =5 \times \frac{\lambda}{9 \times 10^{-6}} \\
& \text { or } \quad \lambda=\frac{12 \times \pi}{180} \times \frac{9 \times 10^{-6}}{5} \mathrm{~m} \\
& \text { or } \quad \lambda=3768 Å
\end{aligned}
$$
So, the wave length of incident light is $\lambda=3768 Å$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
In a biprism experiment, the slit separation is $1 \mathrm{~mm}$. Using monochromatic light
of wavelength $5000 Å$, an interference pattern is obtained on the screen. Where
should the screen be moved, so that the change in fringe width is $12 \cdot 5 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~m}$ ?
options: Away or towards the slit by 25 am
Away or towards the slit by $12 \cdot 5 \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{d}=1 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}$
$\lambda=5000 Å=5 \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{~m}$
$\beta_{2}-\beta_{1}=12.5 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~m}$
$\mathrm{D}_{2}-\mathrm{D}_{1}=?$
$\beta_{1}=\frac{\lambda \mathrm{D}_{1}}{\mathrm{~d}} \quad \beta_{2}=\frac{\lambda \mathrm{D}_{2}}{\mathrm{~d}}$
$\beta_{2}-\beta_{1}=\frac{\lambda}{\mathrm{d}}\left(\mathrm{D}_{2}-\mathrm{D}_{1}\right)$
$\therefore \quad\left(\mathrm{D}_{2}-\mathrm{D}_{1}\right)=\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\lambda}\left(\beta_{2}-\beta_{1}\right)$
$\quad\left(\mathrm{D}_{2}-\mathrm{D}_{1}\right)=\frac{10^{-3}}{5 \times 10^{-7}} \times 12.5 \times 10^{-5}=\frac{12.5}{50}=25 \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Light consisting of a plane waves of wavelength, $\lambda_1=8 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~cm}$ and $\lambda_2=6 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~cm}$ generates an interference pattern in Young's double slit experiment. If $n_1$ denotes the $n_1$ th dark fringe due to light of wavelength $\lambda_1$ which coincides with $n_2$ th bright fringe due to light of wavelength $\lambda_2$, then
questions.solution:
Given, $\lambda_1=8 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~cm}$ and $\lambda_2=6 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~cm}$
Position $n^{\text {h }}$, dark fringe (due to light of wavelength $\lambda_1$ ) is given by,
$x_{n_1}=\left(2 n_1 \times 1\right) \frac{D \lambda_1}{2 d}$
Position of $n_2{ }^{\text {th }}$ bright fringe due to light of wavelength $\lambda_2$ is given by
$x_{n_2}=\frac{n_2 D \lambda_2}{d}$
Since both the fringes are coincide to each other. Hence,
$\begin{aligned}\left(2 n_1+1\right) \frac{D \lambda_1}{2 d} & =\frac{n_2 D \lambda_2}{d} \\ \frac{2 n_1+1}{n_2} & =\frac{2 \lambda_2}{\lambda_1}=\frac{2 \times 6 \times 10^{-5}}{8 \times 10^{-5}} \\ \frac{2 n_1+1}{n_2} & =\frac{3}{2} \\ 4 n_1+2 & =3 n_2 \\ 4 n_1-3 n_2 & =-2 \\ 4 n_1-3 n_2 & =-2\end{aligned}$
when $n_1=1$ and $n_2=2$, then Eq. (i) is satisfied. Hence, for $n_1=1$ and $n_2=2$, given fringes coincide to each other.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two slits separated by a distance of $1 \mathrm{~mm}$ are illuminated with light of wavelength $6.5 \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{~m}$. The interference fringes are observed on a screen placed at $1 \mathrm{~m}$ from the slits. The distance between the third dark fringe and the fifth bright fringe is equal to
questions.solution:
Distance between two slit, $\mathrm{d}=1 \mathrm{~mm}=10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}$ Light of wavelength, $\lambda=6.5 \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{~m}$ Distance of screen from the slit, $D=1 \mathrm{~m}$ For distance of dark fringe,
$$
\mathrm{y}_{\mathrm{n}}=\left(\mathrm{n}-\frac{1}{2}\right) \frac{\lambda \mathrm{D}}{\mathrm{d}}
$$
take $\mathrm{n}=3$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& y_3=\left(3-\frac{1}{2}\right) \frac{6.5 \times 10^{-7} \times 1}{10^{-3}} \\
& =\frac{5}{2} \times 6.5 \times 10^{-4} \\
& =16.25 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~m}
\end{aligned}
$$
For distance of bright fringe, $y_n=\frac{n \lambda D}{d}$ take, $\mathrm{n}=5$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& y_5=\frac{5 \times 6.5 \times 10^{-7} \times 1}{10^{-3}} \\
& =32.5 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~m} \\
& y_5-y_3=32.5 \times 10^{-4}-16.25 \times 10^{-4} \\
& =16.25 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~m} \\
& =1.625 \mathrm{~mm} .
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A Young double slit experimental setup is immersed in water of refractive index <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>33</mn><mo> </mo><mo>.</mo></math> It has slit separation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math> and the distance between slits and screen is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>33</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>. If the wavelength of incident light on slits is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6300</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> then the fringe width on screen is
questions.solution:
The fringe width when Young's double slit experiment setup
is immersed in water,

y=λDμd

Substitute the given values in the above equation.

y=6300×10-10×1.331.33×10-3

y=6.3×10-4 m

y=0.63 mm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A beam of unpolarised light is incident on four polarised plates. Plates are arranged so that each plate direction is at an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo>°</mo></math> with its previous one. What is the percentage of intensity of light coming out of final polariser-
questions.solution:
Intensity coming out from the first sheet, I1=I02

Intensity coming out from the second sheet,
I2=I1cos230°=I02×322

⇒I2=I02×34

⇒I2=38I0

Similarly, I3=I2cos230°=38I0×34=932I0

I4=27128I0

Now, I4I0=27128

⇒I4I0×100=27128×100=21.09%
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: What will be the limit of resolution of a microscope if its numerical aperture is 0.12 and wavelength of light used is 6000 Å?
questions.solution:
Given: Numerical aperture NA=0.12, wavelength λ=6000 Å.

The expression of the limit of resolution of a microscope is given as, ∆d=λ2μsinθ, here μ is the refractive index and θ is half-angle subtended.

We know that the numerical aperture is equal to μsinθ.

Thus, the limit of resolution, ∆d=6000×10-102×0.12=25×10-7≈30.5×10-7 m.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The angular width of the central maximum in the Fraunhofer's diffraction pattern is measured. The slit is illuminated by the light of wavelength <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6000</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>∘</mo></mover></math> . If the slit is illuminated by the light of another wavelength, angular width decreases by <math><mn>30</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">%</mi></math> . The wavelength of light used is
questions.solution:
For the first diffraction minimum, dsin⁡θ=λ
And if the angle is small, sin⁡θ=θ
dθ=λ
i.e., Half angular width, θ=λd
Full angular width w=2θ=2λd
Also, w'=2λ'd
∴λ'λ=w'w or λ'=λw'w

=6000×0.7=4200 Å
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In standard Young's double-slit experiment, visible light λ∈350nm, 750nm is used in both the slits. Distance between the slits is 2 mm, and the distance of the screen from the slits is 1 m. A thin glass slab μ = 1.5  of thickness 6 μm is placed in front of slit S2. Which among the following wavelengths is missing at point P, which is directly in front of slit S2?(Neglect dispersion of light)
questions.solution:
Net path difference = μ - 1 t - yd D

=32-16 μm-d/2dD=1 μm

For Destructive interference

Δx=1 μm=2n+1λ2⇒λ=22n+1 μm

λ = 2000 nm 2 + 1 = 2000 3            ( n=1 )

λ=2000 nm4+1=20005nm=400 nm      ( n=2 )
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two slits separated by $0.5 \mathrm{~mm}$ are illuminated by light of wavelength $500 \mathrm{~nm}$. The screen is at a distance of $120 \mathrm{~cm}$ from the slits. The phase difference between the interfering waves at a point $3 \mathrm{~mm}$ on the screen from the central bright fringe is ........... .
questions.solution:
Given,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{d}=0.5 \times 10^{-9} \mathrm{~m} \text { and } \gamma=3 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~mm} \\
& \lambda=500 \times 10^{-9} \mathrm{~m} \\
& D=120 \mathrm{~cm}=120 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~m} \\
& \gamma=\frac{\Delta x D}{d}
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\Delta x=\frac{\gamma d}{D}
$$
$$
\Delta x=\frac{3 \times 10^{-3} \times 0.5 \times 10^{-3}}{1.2}=\frac{5}{4} \times 10^{-6}
$$
$$
\Delta \phi=\frac{2 \pi \times 1.25 \times 10^{-6}}{500 \times 10^{-9}}=5 \pi
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In a Young's double slit experiment, slits are separated by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>, and the screen is placed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>150</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> away. A beam of light consisting of two wavelengths, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>650</mn><mo> </mo><mi>nm</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>520</mn><mo> </mo><mi>nm</mi></math>, is used to obtain interference fringes on the screen. The least distance from the common central maximum to the point where the bright fringes due to both the wavelengths coincide is:
questions.solution:
y1=n1λ1Dd  for bright fringes

y2=n2λ2Dd for bright fringes

To coincide

n1λ1=n2λ2

n1×650=n2×520

∴    n1n2=45

For minimum value of n1 and n2

n1=4 n2=5

y1min=4×650×nm×150m0.5×10-3m

=7800×10-6m

=7.8 mm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Assertion: An excessively thin film appears dark in reflected light.

Reason: The film absorbs selected radiations falling on it.
options: Assertion is True, Reason is True; Reason is a correct explanation for Assertion
Assertion is True, Reason is True; Reason is not a correct explanation for Assertion
Assertion is True, Reason is False
Assertion is False but, Reason is True.
questions.solution:
This phenomenon occurs because of the interference of the two light waves. One from the top surface of the film that got reflected and the other from the second surface. The light from the second surface goes total internal reflection.

The two light waves have path difference of λ2 when t<<λ which is destructive interference and hence the film appears dark.

Hence, Assertion is True, Reason is False.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Consider the following statements about interference of light
A. The interference fringes are equally bright and equally spaced
B. At the centre of a bright fringe, the intensity is four times the intensity of the incident wave
C. For constructive interference of two waves, the crest of one wave coincides with trough of another wave.
Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.solution:
In interference, the fringes are equally bright and equally spaced and intensity of the bright fringe is four times the intensity of each incident wave.
Resultant intensity at a point is given by
$I=I_1+I_2+2 \sqrt{I_1} \sqrt{I_2} \cos \delta$
where, $I_1$ is the intensity of the wave from source $1, I_2$ is the intensity of the wave from source 2 . No two sources in nature can be coherent. So, sources are made from one source by either splitting the amplitude or the wave fronts, by means of double-slit or Fresnel's prism or Lloyd's mirror etc. Further, to get a high contrast $I_1=I_2=I$.
So, for constructive interference and at the central maxima $\delta=0$,
$I_{\max }=2 I+2 I \cos \delta=2 I(1+\cos \delta)=4 I \cos ^2\left(\frac{\delta}{2}\right)=4 I$
So at central maxima intensity is four times the incident wave.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A thin slice is cut out of a glass cylinder along a plane parallel to its axis. The slice is placed on a flat glass plate as shown in figure. The observed interference fringes from this combination shall be
options: having fringe spacing which increases as we go outwards
questions.solution:
Locus of equal path difference are lines running parallel to the axis of the cylinder. Hence, straight fringes will be observed.

Note: Circular rings (also called Newton's rings) are observed in interference pattern when a planoconvex lens of large focal length is placed with its convex surface in contact with a plane glass plate because locus of equal path difference in this case is a circle.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The two coherent sources with intensity ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> produce interference. The fringe visibility will be
questions.solution:
We know that :

Fringe visibility=Imax-IminImax+Imin

β=a1a2

Substituting the values from question:

=a1+a22−a1−a22a1+a22+a1−a22=4a1a22a12+a22

=2a1a2a1a22+1=2ββ+1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two radio antennas radiating waves in phase are located at points A and B, 200 m apart (Figure). The radio waves have a frequency of 6 MHz. A radio receiver is moved out from point B along a line perpendicular to the line connecting A and B (line BC as shown in the figure). At what distances from B will there be destructive interference?
questions.solution:
C = nλ                  ⇒ λ = C n

λ = 3 × 10 8 6 × 10 6                   ⇒ λ = 50m

path difference at P = AP - BP

path difference at P = 4 λ 2 + x 2 - x  ..............(1)

For destructive interference at P

path difference = (2n -1) λ 2  ...............(2)

From (1) and (2)

4 λ 2 + x 2 - x = 2n - 1 λ 2

4 λ 2 + x 2 = 2n - 1 λ 2 + x

For first minima n = 1,

x 1 = 6 3 λ 4     ⇒ x 1 = 787.5 m

For second minima n = 2,

x 2 = 5 5 λ 1 2     ⇒ x 2 = 2 2 9 m

For third minima n = 3,

x 3 = 3 9 λ 2 0     ⇒ x 3 = 9 7 . 5 m
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A diffraction pattern is obtained by making blue light incident on a narrow slit. If blue light is replaced by red light then
options: there is no change in diffraction pattern.
questions.solution:
The correct option is (C).
Concept Based: The width of the diffraction bands is directly proportional to the wavelength thus, a minor broadening is observed.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: When Young's double slit experiment is performed in air, the Y-coordinates of central maxima and $10^{\text {th }}$ maxima are $2 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $5 \mathrm{~cm}$ respectively. If the apparatus is immersed in a liquid of refractive index 1.5 , the corresponding Y-coordinates will be
questions.solution:
For YDSE apparatus in air
For central maxima
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{y_0 d}{D}-d \sin \theta=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \sin \theta=\frac{y_0}{D}=\frac{2}{D} ... (i)
\end{aligned}$
For $10^{\text {th }}$ maxima
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{\mathrm{y}_{10} \mathrm{~d}}{\mathrm{D}}-\mathrm{d} \sin \theta=10 \lambda \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{5 \mathrm{~d}}{\mathrm{D}}-\mathrm{d} \sin \theta=10 \lambda
\end{aligned}$
From equation (i), we have
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{5 d}{D}-\frac{2 d}{D}=10 \lambda \\
& \Rightarrow 10 \lambda=\frac{3 d}{D} \Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{3 d}{10 D}
\end{aligned}$
Now, when the YDSE apparatus is immersed in a liquid of refractive index 1.5 , then it's wave length $\lambda^{\prime}=\frac{\lambda}{1.5}$
As the y coordinate of central maximum doesn't depend upon wavelength, therefore it will remain same as in air, $\mathrm{y}_0^{\prime}=\mathrm{y}_0=2 \mathrm{~cm}$
For $10^{\text {th }}$ maxina
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{\mathrm{y}_{10}^{\prime} \mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{D}}-\mathrm{d} \sin \theta=10 \lambda^{\prime} \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{y}_{10}^{\prime} \mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{D}}-\frac{\mathrm{d} \times 2}{\mathrm{D}}=\frac{10 \times 3 \mathrm{~d}}{1.510 \mathrm{D}} \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{y}_{10}^{\prime}=4 \mathrm{~cm}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In Young's double slit experiment the intensity at a point on the screen is $K$, where path difference is $\lambda$. What will be the intensity at the point where path difference is $\frac{\lambda}{4}$ ?
questions.solution:
$I=2 I_0(1+\cos \phi)$
$\phi=\frac{2 \pi x}{\lambda}$, where $x$ is the path as difference.
$\therefore K=2 I_0\left(1+\cos \left(\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda} \times \lambda\right)\right)=4 I_0$
Now, for path difference $\frac{\lambda}{4}$
$\begin{aligned}
& K^{\prime}=2 I_0\left(1+\cos \left(\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda} \times \frac{\lambda}{4}\right)\right)=2 I_0 \\
& \therefore K^{\prime}=\frac{K}{2}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
